mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes that allows you to back up your cluster resources and persistent volumes, and migrate them across clusters. It is particularly useful for disaster recovery and data migration scenarios. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration of cloud storage backends and potential complexity in managing backups and restores.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This repository contains the Velero Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Velero CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Velero to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the CRDs needed for Velero.

steps.title: Install Velero using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Velero on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --version 1.18.0 --namespace velero --create-namespace --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-velero-bucket' with your actual S3 bucket name.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --version 1.18.0 --namespace velero --create-namespace --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

steps.title: Verify Velero installation
steps.description:
This step checks if Velero is installed and running correctly in your cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> You should see the Velero server pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step removes Velero from your cluster. Note that this will delete all backups and associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This command will remove the Velero release.
> This command will delete the Velero namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Velero configuration and data. This step will upgrade Velero to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get backups.velero.io --all-namespaces

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --version 1.18.0 --namespace velero

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> Check for existing backups before proceeding.
> This command will upgrade Velero to the specified version.
> Verify that the Velero pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup: failed to create backup storage location'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that Velero cannot access the specified backup storage location.

**Fix:**
Check your bucket permissions and ensure that the bucket exists in the specified region.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in velero namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that Velero is not installed correctly or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that Velero is installed in the 'velero' namespace and check the installation logs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to restore: no backup found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when trying to restore from a backup that does not exist.

**Fix:**
Verify that the backup exists by running 'kubectl get backups.velero.io --all-namespaces'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create volume snapshot'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the volume snapshot location is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the configuration for the volume snapshot location and ensure it is set up correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for additional cloud providers, improved backup performance, and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy backup storage locations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17, ensure to update your backup storage location configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero server pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n velero'.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --version 1.18.0 --namespace velero --create-namespace --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=us-west-2 --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
